rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANA TOM SHARE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement Probate Code 17200(B)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) A copy of the settlement agreement sought to be approved by the Court was not attached to the proposed order. See LRSF 14.7(A)(2) & (3)("A proposed order must be complete in itself in that it must set forth, with the same particularity required of a judgment in a civil matter. A proposed order must be drafted so that its general effect may be determined without reference to the petition on which it is based."). No proposed order has been received from any objector. 2) Diana Tom's objection filed on 05/30/2023 was not signed and verified. 3) Jacqueline Tom must sign her objection (in the e-filing queue as of noon on 06/29/2023), as she appears to reside in the county where the office of one of her attorneys of record is located. See CRC 7.103(a) & (c). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUNE PUTNAM GOLDSMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/31/2023, as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 06/05/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The status report (petition) must be verified by the administrator. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 4) The box at item 1 of the DE-120 form must be checked. 5) Direct notice must be given to each minor beneficiary, as this is not a protective proceeding. See CRC 7.51(d). 6) If the decedent's Chase bank accounts listed on the inventory and appraisal turned out to be joint accounts not belonging to this estate, a corrected inventory and appraisal must be filed, with a copy served on the person who requested special notice. See Probate Code section 1252(b). 7) No DE-174 form has been filed in response to the creditor claim. 8) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided. 9) The petition must be amended and renoticed to pray for a continuance of administration for a specific period. See LRSF 14.35(J)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Statement Of Interest And Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interests In Estate And Authorizing Distribution Directly To Celtic Research
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/07/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 06/12/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Direct notice must be given to the personal representative. See CRC 7.51(a). 4) Direct notice must be given to Bridge Financial Funding LLC as a person who requested special notice, in addition to a separate copy given to its counsel. 5) Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must be served on all persons who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. 6) The petition must be signed by an authorized representative of Celtic Research. See CRC 7.103(a). 7) Is the relief sought by this petition premature, given that no accountings have been filed? See Estate of Ramsey (1951) 107 Cal.App.2d 372, 375 [Clearly it is within the discretion of the court to conduct such hearings in advance as well as at the time of the hearing of a petition for distribution. However, in the present case, the hearing was had prior to the filing of any account by the executor, although it was necessary for the court to know the value of the interests of appellants in the residuary estate in order to determine whether the sum they had received for their assignments was fair or grossly inadequate. We think that where interests have been assigned outright the court might properly refuse to conduct a hearing under section 1020.1 in advance of a hearing upon an account of the executor or administrator.]; see also Estate of Boyd (1979) 98 Cal.App.3d 125, 134 ["The import of the holding in Ramsey is that the court must have some reliable foundation on which to base its evaluation of the property. Where no executor's account had been filed, the court "might properly refuse to conduct a hearing under section 1020.1."]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH A. LEAF
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/07/2023. The following procedural issues need to be cleared two weeks prior. (1) Caption sets forth Distribution to Testamentary Trust; however, petition does not pray accordingly. CRC 7.102. (2) Paragraph 27 states that the estate's funds are kept in a someone else's Trust (the Joan Leaf 1987 Revocable Trust) account. If the estate was entitled to the trust's distribution, the funds should have been kept in this estate's account. Appear Administrator has not transfer the funds to the estate but kept it in in the Trust which is not in this court jurisdiction. The funds should be transferred to the estate account. Otherwise, petition to provides an authority under which the court can order the funds in someone else's trust (the Joan Leaf 1987 Revocable Trust) that is not before this court jurisdiction to be distributed to the heir of the estate of Elizabeth A. Leaf. (3) Funds to be deposited to meet FDIC requirement. (4) Petition to be amended to allege that assets on hand are all being held in the estate of Elizabeth A. Leaf meeting the FDIC requirement, and the proposed distribution of the assets properly held in the name of the estate to be distributed. (5) Proposed Order to be revised accordingly. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
